backgrounds.name: The Publish-or-Perish Lair (Portrait)
backgrounds.concept: Abyss lich sanctum with crystal phylactery housing at the center, publication-records carved into the walls as a mausoleum of achievement, the desperate geometry of a space where the occupant refused to accept ending.
backgrounds.prompt: pixel art dungeon room, front-facing perspective at eye level looking straight into the chamber, NOT isometric NOT top-down NOT angled, a dark stone archway exit at the exact center of the back wall leading deeper into shadow — this doorway is the focal vanishing point of the composition, the room has strong depth perspective with a visible stone floor receding toward the back wall, at least 3 depth layers — foreground props near the camera then middle ground furniture and equipment then the back wall with the archway, the stone archway exit door is fully visible and centered at approximately 40% from the top of the image and 50% horizontal, the archway is always the same size relative to the frame — about 20% of image width and 25% of image height, all room elements are arranged on either side of this central passage leaving a clear corridor from the doorway to the foreground, to the left a massive printing press made of dark iron and bone churning out parchment scrolls with crystal phylacteries mounted above it — each one a sealed glass coffin containing a preserved scroll — and tall stone obelisks carved like tombstones with rune tally marks carved into their faces, the left wall densely covered from floor to ceiling with chains of dangling dried ink bottles on iron hooks and pinned rejection notices and failed manuscript pages wedged into every crevice and moss creeping between the stones, to the right a sacrificial stone altar for peer review surrounded by crystal display pedestals and towering stacks of half-finished drafts pinned to every surface with iron pins and a bookcase crammed with bound volumes spilling onto the floor, the right wall packed from floor to ceiling with iron shelves overflowing with rejected manuscripts and crumpled parchment balls stuffed into cracks and rune-covered correction notices pinned in overlapping layers, the floor is cracked stone tiles covered in discarded crumpled manuscripts that were not good enough with dried ink stains in dark pools and scattered bone-white parchment debris everywhere, the ceiling has iron candelabra brackets and heavy cobwebs stretched between hanging phylactery chains and dangling dried ink bottles on iron hooks, the room tells the story of a publish-or-perish necropolis — desperate creative energy that never stops producing, churning out work until the end, every surface wall floor and ceiling is dense with objects and texture and visual interest, the center corridor from doorway to foreground is completely clear of furniture and objects, crisp clean pixel art with visible individual pixels, 16-bit JRPG dungeon style, NOT painterly NOT smooth NOT illustrated NOT watercolor, hard pixel edges, limited color palette per object, dithering for shading, rich detail that rewards close inspection, absolutely no readable text no letters no numbers no words no symbols that resemble writing no signs no inscriptions, all markings must be abstract runes or illegible scrawl, 9:16 vertical composition with detail filling the full vertical space from floor to ceiling, absolutely no characters no figures no creatures no people no monsters, no UI elements
